features.summary: Goldman Sachs delivered record 2021 results with revenues of $59 billion and net earnings of $21.6 billion, representing a 23% ROE. Investment Banking had an extraordinary year with record revenues exceeding the previous high by over $5 billion, driven by strong M&A activity where Goldman maintained its #1 position. Global Markets generated $22 billion in revenues, up 4% year-over-year, with improved client relationships as the firm now ranks top 3 with 72 of the top 100 clients. Asset Management and Consumer & Wealth Management both achieved record results, with firm-wide assets under supervision reaching $2.5 trillion.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued strong M&A activity driven by supply chain disruptions forcing strategic repositioning and consolidation opportunities. The firm anticipates benefits from rising interest rates given its modest asset-sensitive position, with expectations of four Fed rate hikes in 2022. Goldman plans to provide updated strategic targets and goals next month, building on progress made since the 2020 Investor Day. The company remains committed to its efficiency ratio target of 60% and expects to continue investing in technology while maintaining discipline on expenses in different market environments.
features.risks: Management highlighted significant uncertainty from the Omicron variant contributing to market volatility, though they expect less economic impact than previous waves. Persistent inflation and supply chain pressures may intensify before decreasing, potentially impacting economic growth and asset prices. The unwinding of easy monetary policies could create more volatility affecting client activity levels. There are also headwinds to the capital position from pending acquisitions and other deployment opportunities, with expectations for continued pressure on capital ratios.
features.key_drivers: Record performance was driven by exceptional client activity across Investment Banking, particularly in M&A where Goldman advised on over $1.8 trillion of announced transactions. The firm's strategic focus on client relationships in Global Markets resulted in significant wallet share gains and improved positioning. Strong market conditions and the firm's ability to capture opportunities through nimble capital allocation contributed to the 23% ROE. Growth in fee-based revenues from Asset Management and Wealth Management, along with expansion of the consumer platform to over 10 million customers, supported the diversification strategy.
